changes_applied.change: added a narrow metadata-line exclusion so the engine no longer reports its own rule-definition and taint-metadata blocks during self-scan
added regression coverage ensuring skipped self-scan metadata lines do not produce findings
updated filename sanitization to preserve safe relative repository paths while still stripping traversal and unsafe characters
added focused regression coverage for router filename sanitization and API scan behavior
stopped truncating scanned source files and added filename context to each SAST code-scan request
added a small direct-engine helper used to confirm the exact self-scan findings during discrepancy diagnosis
rewrote the branch status narrative to reflect the corrected self-scan path, fresh evidence, and the newly surfaced broader backlog
wrote a new machine-readable cycle report for the validated SAST self-scan fidelity remediation pass

environment_notes.known_nonfatal_test_message: Failed to export metrics batch code: 404, reason: Not Found
environment_notes.observed_self_scan_warning: AutoFix step emitted a 500 warning for insecure deserialization even though the overall self-scan summary reported success
environment_notes.result_artifact_hygiene: the self-scan JSON artifact path under data/demo-results is reused and lacks a trustworthy run-specific timestamp field, so the autonomous-cycle log is the authoritative evidence artifact for cycle identity
environment_notes.omni_assessment: external Omni tooling may be selectively trialed as a sandbox-local helper, but it is not currently recommended as a primary workflow accelerator in this environment because Homebrew is unavailable and the session model is not a normal interactive terminal-hook loop

remaining_risks: the refreshed autonomous self-scan now surfaces a broader real or plausibly real SAST backlog across multiple product modules and needs triage rather than suppression
the self-scan still reports an AutoFix 500 warning while the overall summary reports 17/17 passed
the self-scan JSON artifact path remains non-unique and weakens machine-readable evidence traceability between runs
root Dockerfile scan still surfaces package-pinning and cleanup findings that remain unresolved
local telemetry export still emits a non-fatal 404 message during validation
